proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +34.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is 100% Choppy with UP_TRI dominating 24/34 open positions; if regime shifts to Bear, the deferred Tier M boost will fire on an already UP_TRI-heavy book and amplify single-signal concentration
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review); cohort R may shift materially if exit rules change
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +34.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +34.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with 100% Choppy exposure across 34 open positions, so activation is deferred and existing concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-19) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +27.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
UP_TRI already dominates open book (24/34 = 70.6%); even narrow Metal-filtered TAKE_SMALL adds to a signal-type concentration that compounds with the 100% Choppy regime concentration
Sample bias on n=17 cannot be ruled out from current view; if Metal cohort clusters on a few sessions, Wilson lower bound overstates durability
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +27.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +27.9pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies UP_TRI exposure (24 of 34 open) in a 100% Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-19) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime diversifies via natural Day-6 exits dropping Choppy share below 0.80, or (b) Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M. If unwilling to pause, hedge net long bias with index puts sized to ~24 UP_TRI positions (Bank and Metal sectors are the two largest exposures, hedge via BankNifty puts and Metal sector short).
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull regime flip leaves the 24 UP_TRI longs without their thesis tailwind (mean-reversion fails as trend persists); a Choppy→Bear flip simultaneously unwinds all 24 longs while the 6 DOWN_TRI shorts cannot offset the loss. Either way, the entire 34-position book reprices on a single regime variable with no statistical edge support.

evidence: All 34 open positions sit in Choppy regime
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort is Candidate tier only (n=118) — unvalidated edge
UP_TRI dominates signal mix at 24/34 = 70.6% (below 85% but reinforces directional skew)
Top cells UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank (14.71%) and UP_TRI×Choppy×Metal (14.71%) below 20% cell threshold but both map to Candidate-tier cohort
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime diversifies via natural Day-6 exits dropping Choppy share below 0.80, or (b) Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M. If unwilling to pause, hedge net long bias with index puts sized to ~24 UP_TRI positions (Bank and Metal sectors are the two largest exposures, hedge via BankNifty puts and Metal sector short).
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull regime flip leaves the 24 UP_TRI longs without their thesis tailwind (mean-reversion fails as trend persists); a Choppy→Bear flip simultaneously unwinds all 24 longs while the 6 DOWN_TRI shorts cannot offset the loss. Either way, the entire 34-position book reprices on a single regime variable with no statistical edge support.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above the 0.80 threshold) and the matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=118) — unvalidated edge. No single cell breaches 20% (top cells at 14.71%) and UP_TRI share at 24/34 = 70.6% is below 85%, but the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn — a Choppy regime flip would unwind the entire 34-position book simultaneously with no statistical justification.
